name: Rek'Sai
playstyleSummary: Rek'Sai is a jungler who alternates between burrowed and un-burrowed forms, using tunnels to zip around the map and a powerful bite that gets stronger as she builds Fury. She's a strong pick for players who like fast jungle clears and map-wide mobility over standing and slugging it out.
strengths: Very fast jungle clear and early ganks
Burrow grants increased move speed and warns her of nearby enemies
Tunnels let her cross the map quickly once placed
Void Rush lets her dodge damage briefly while lunging at a marked target
weaknesses: Loses her basic attacks and has reduced vision while burrowed
Tunnels can be destroyed by enemies standing on the entrances
Furious Bite's true damage bonus requires building up full Fury first
Less effective if enemies deny her tunnel network

abilities.name: Fury of the Xer'Sai
abilities.howToUseIt: You build Fury by attacking and landing basic abilities, then consume it while burrowed to heal - burrow between fights or camps to recover health using your stored Fury.

abilities.name: Queen's Wrath / Prey Seeker
abilities.howToUseIt: While un-burrowed, your next 3 attacks deal bonus damage to enemies nearby; while burrowed, this instead launches a burst that damages and reveals enemies in the area.

abilities.name: Burrow / Un-burrow
abilities.howToUseIt: Burrow into the ground for increased move speed, reduced vision, and new abilities, but you lose the ability to basic attack - burrow to travel safely and unburrow when you're ready to fight.

abilities.name: Furious Bite / Tunnel
abilities.howToUseIt: While un-burrowed, bite your target for physical damage, or true damage instead if your Fury is full; while burrowed, place a long-lasting tunnel you can use to travel - spread your tunnels around the map for options later.

abilities.name: Void Rush
abilities.howToUseIt: Mark enemies by damaging them, then activate to become briefly untargetable and lunge at a marked target for heavy damage based on their max health - save it to punish or finish a high-health target you've already damaged.

earlyGamePlan: Start your jungle clear un-burrowed for the bonus attack damage from Queen's Wrath, then burrow between camps to heal off your built-up Fury and move faster. Place tunnels around the map early so you have travel options later, and look to gank a lane once you've got a full clear or two under your belt.
quickTips: Burrow between jungle camps to heal using your stored Fury
Place tunnels in useful spots early - you'll want travel options later in the game
Save Furious Bite for when your Fury is full to get the true damage bonus
Use Void Rush to finish off a high-health target you've already marked with damage
Remember you can't basic attack while burrowed, so unburrow before a straight fight
whatToExpect: Rek'Sai can travel the map quickly using tunnels and her burrowed move speed, so don't assume she's far away just because you don't see her - destroy any tunnel entrances you find by standing on them. Be careful when your health is high, since Void Rush lets her briefly dodge damage and lunge at you for a hit based on your maximum health, so try to avoid getting marked by her damage in the first place.

glossary.definition: The role and area between lanes filled with monsters that junglers fight for gold and experience.
When a jungler leaves their area to ambush an enemy in a lane.
Damage that ignores armor and magic resist, so it always deals its full value.
A state where a champion cannot be selected as the target of attacks or most abilities.
A debuff placed on an enemy that enables or boosts a follow-up effect.
